extraction.one_line_summary: RUBIO Einer won a 75km shortened mountain stage after attacking from the break multiple times on the final climb to Crans-Montana, beating PINOT by 6 seconds.
tactical_patterns: Thibaut PINOT attacked repeatedly from the break on final climb but couldn't sustain sprint finish, losing despite animating most.
CARTHY Hugh attacked solo from GC group to claw back time, riding away from FORTUNATO while sitting outside top-10.
Break infighting over workload in valley roads before final climb suggests Movistar-Cepeda/Pinot alliance issues influenced late dynamics.
CEPEDA Alexander still working from break despite teammate CARTHY attacking from behind, unusual dual-team tactic for time gains.

rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: dominated animation with most attacks but couldn't finish sprint in final grand tour, suggesting tactics exceed finishing power
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: positive: attacked solo from GC group sitting outside top-10, shows form recovery despite earlier struggles
rider_signals.FORTUNATO Lorenzo: negative: dropped by CARTHY despite attacking first from GC group, weaker than positioning suggested
rider_signals.LEKNESSUND Andreas: negative: lost 7 seconds in Maglia Rosa despite short 75km stage, vulnerability on extreme weather days exposed
rider_signals.SWIFT Ben: positive: rode fantastically uphill, downhill, and flat protecting Maglia Rosa, unexpected climbing strength for classics rider
